articles.headline: Viksit Bharat 2047: Government Unveils National Infrastructure & Logistics Corridor for Economic Transformation
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced a flagship National Infrastructure & Logistics Corridor project, a key pillar of the 'Viksit Bharat 2047' vision.
• This multi-modal corridor aims to integrate industrial hubs, economic zones, and port facilities across several states, significantly reducing logistics costs.
• Focus areas include high-speed rail, dedicated freight corridors, national highways, and greenfield industrial cities.
• The project is expected to generate massive employment opportunities and boost manufacturing, trade, and exports.
• Public-private partnerships (PPPs) will be crucial for the project's financing and execution, with a new regulatory framework being introduced.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Infrastructure: Energy, Ports, Roads, Airports, Railways etc.
Investment models
prelimsPoints: 'Viksit Bharat 2047' vision
National Infrastructure & Logistics Corridor project
Focus on reducing logistics costs
Integration of industrial hubs, economic zones, ports
Expected to boost manufacturing, trade, employment
mainsPoints: Significance of infrastructure development for economic growth and competitiveness
Challenges and opportunities in implementing large-scale multi-modal projects
Role of PPPs in funding and execution of national projects
Impact on regional development, employment generation, and socio-economic indicators
Alignment with national goals like 'Make in India' and export promotion
articles.imageDescription: An infographic illustrating the proposed National Infrastructure & Logistics Corridor, highlighting key routes and economic zones.
articles.trivia: India aims to bring down its logistics costs from the current 13-14% of GDP to less than 10% by 2030, a key objective of such corridor projects.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Revisit Scope of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) for Mega Projects
articles.explainer:
• A special bench of the Supreme Court has decided to re-examine the legal framework and implementation of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) for major infrastructure and industrial projects.
• The move comes amidst growing concerns over the efficacy of current EIA processes in balancing development with environmental protection.
• Key issues to be addressed include public consultation mechanisms, post-clearance compliance monitoring, and the role of expert appraisal committees.
• Environmental activists and legal experts have welcomed the decision, hoping for stronger safeguards against ecological degradation.
• The ruling could lead to significant reforms in India's environmental governance and project approval procedures.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies
Environmental pollution and degradation
prelimsPoints: EIA process and its statutory basis (Environment Protection Act, 1986)
Role of Supreme Court in environmental jurisprudence
Components of EIA: screening, scoping, public hearing, appraisal
Difference between EIA and Strategic Environmental Assessment (SEA)
mainsPoints: Critically analyze the effectiveness of EIA in India as a tool for sustainable development.
Discuss the challenges in implementing EIA, including issues of transparency, public participation, and post-clearance compliance.
Examine the balance between developmental imperatives and environmental conservation, with reference to judicial interventions.
Suggest reforms to strengthen the EIA process in India.
articles.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents, symbolizing a judicial review of environmental policies.
articles.trivia: The first formal EIA notification in India was issued in 1994, under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.

articles.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Enhanced Defence & Space Cooperation
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in Paris, building on the momentum from recent high-level visits, including the Republic Day guest.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a robust strategic partnership, focusing on deepening cooperation in defence, space, and emerging technologies.
• Discussions included joint military exercises, co-development of defence platforms, and intelligence sharing to counter regional security threats.
• In space, new agreements were signed for collaboration on satellite navigation, earth observation, and human spaceflight missions.
• The dialogue also emphasized strengthening bilateral ties in trade, climate action, and multilateral forums like the UN Security Council.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
prelimsPoints: India-France strategic partnership established in 1998
Key pillars: defence, space, nuclear energy
Joint military exercises: Shakti (Army), Varuna (Navy), Garuda (Air Force)
France's role as a major defence supplier to India
Collaboration in EU context and UN Security Council
mainsPoints: Analyze the significance of India-France strategic partnership in India's pursuit of strategic autonomy and its Indo-Pacific strategy.
Discuss the evolution of defence and space cooperation between India and France, highlighting key achievements and future prospects.
Evaluate how this partnership contributes to regional stability and addresses emerging security challenges.
Examine the role of France as a reliable partner for India in a multipolar world, distinct from other major powers.
articles.imageDescription: Flags of India and France displayed prominently during a diplomatic meeting.
articles.trivia: France was the first P5 nation to support India's claim for a permanent seat in the UN Security Council.

articles.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Cryogenic Engine for Future Heavy-Lift Missions
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful hot test of a new, more powerful cryogenic engine at its Propulsion Complex in Mahendragiri.
• This advanced engine is designed to power the upper stages of India's next generation heavy-lift launch vehicles, significantly enhancing payload capacity.
• The successful test marks a crucial step towards developing more efficient and cost-effective access to space for India.
• It will be instrumental for future missions, including advanced communication satellites, deep-space probes, and potentially crewed missions beyond low-Earth orbit.
• The development underscores India's growing self-reliance and technological prowess in complex rocketry and space propulsion systems.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Cryogenic engines use liquid oxygen and liquid hydrogen as propellants.
ISRO's previous cryogenic engine was used in GSLV Mk III (LVM3).
Significance for heavier satellites and interplanetary missions.
Mahendragiri: ISRO Propulsion Complex (IPRC) location.
Difference between solid, liquid, and cryogenic propellants.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous cryogenic engine development for India's space program and strategic autonomy.
Analyze how enhanced heavy-lift capabilities will impact India's role in global space economy and exploration.
Examine the technological challenges involved in cryogenic engine development and how ISRO has overcome them.
Evaluate the potential applications of this new engine for future Indian space missions, including human spaceflight and deep-space exploration.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration of a cryogenic engine firing, with flames emanating from the nozzle against a dark background.
articles.trivia: India is one of only a handful of nations to have successfully developed and deployed cryogenic rocket technology.

articles.headline: Economic Survey 2024-25 Projects Robust 7% Growth, Highlights Global Headwinds
articles.explainer:
• The Economic Survey 2024-25, tabled ahead of the Union Budget, projected a robust 7% GDP growth for the upcoming fiscal year.
• The Survey highlighted strong domestic demand, government capital expenditure, and a resilient services sector as key growth drivers.
• It also cautioned about persistent global headwinds, including geopolitical tensions, commodity price volatility, and slower global trade growth.
• Recommendations included continued focus on structural reforms, fiscal consolidation, and boosting private investment to sustain growth trajectory.
• The document emphasized the need for targeted policies to address inflation, create employment, and ensure equitable development.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey is prepared by the Chief Economic Adviser and presented before the Budget.
It reviews the economic performance of the past year and provides an outlook for the future.
Key indicators: GDP growth, inflation, fiscal deficit, CAD, FDI.
Difference between nominal and real GDP.
Components of GDP: C+I+G+(X-M).
mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers and challenges to India's economic growth as highlighted by the Economic Survey 2024-25.
Discuss the policy recommendations of the Survey, particularly regarding fiscal management and structural reforms, in achieving sustainable and inclusive growth.
Evaluate the impact of global economic uncertainties and geopolitical developments on India's growth trajectory.
Examine the role of government capital expenditure and private investment in boosting economic activity and employment generation.
articles.imageDescription: A chart showing an upward trend of India's GDP growth, with economic indicators in the background.
articles.trivia: The first Economic Survey was presented in 1950-51 and was presented along with the Union Budget until 1964, after which it was delinked.

articles.headline: India Launches National Mission on Green Hydrogen for Industrial Decarbonisation
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy officially launched the National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting industrial decarbonisation and energy security.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, leveraging its renewable energy potential.
• Key objectives include developing domestic manufacturing capabilities for electrolysers, promoting research and development, and creating demand for green hydrogen in sectors like refineries, fertilizers, and steel.
• Significant financial incentives and policy support will be provided for pilot projects and scaling up production capacity.
• This initiative is crucial for India to meet its climate change commitments and achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Infrastructure: Energy
articles.source: Ministry of New and Renewable Energy
prelimsPoints: Green hydrogen is produced using renewable energy (electrolysis of water).
Difference between green, grey, and blue hydrogen.
Applications: fuel for transport, industrial feedstock, energy storage.
India's target for green hydrogen production.
Role of National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving climate targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges in establishing India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Examine the policy measures and technological advancements required to scale up green hydrogen adoption in key industrial sectors.
Evaluate the economic and environmental impact of the National Green Hydrogen Mission on India's energy transition.
articles.imageDescription: An illustration depicting a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
articles.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but it rarely exists as a free element on Earth.

articles.headline: Health Ministry Expands 'Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission' to Remote Rural Areas
articles.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry announced a significant expansion of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to cover previously underserved remote rural areas.
• The initiative aims to create a seamless online platform for healthcare services, enabling digital health records, teleconsultations, and paperless prescriptions.
• Special emphasis is being placed on training local community health workers and equipping primary health centers with necessary digital infrastructure.
• The expansion seeks to bridge the urban-rural divide in healthcare access and improve the efficiency of health service delivery.
• This move is crucial for achieving universal health coverage and improving public health outcomes across the nation.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Issues relating to development and management of Social Sector/Services relating to Health, Education, Human Resources
prelimsPoints: ABDM launched in 2021, aims to create a digital health ecosystem.
Key components: Health ID, Healthcare Professionals Registry (HPR), Health Facility Registry (HFR).
Nodal Ministry: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare.
Benefits: accessibility, efficiency, data security.
Role of private sector in ABDM.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of digital health initiatives like ABDM in transforming healthcare delivery in rural India.
Analyze the challenges involved in implementing digital health solutions in remote areas, including digital literacy, infrastructure, and data privacy concerns.
Examine how ABDM can contribute to achieving the goals of universal health coverage and strengthening primary healthcare.
Suggest measures to ensure equitable access and effective utilization of digital health services across diverse socio-economic strata.
articles.imageDescription: A digital illustration showing a healthcare worker assisting a patient with a tablet, symbolizing digital health services in a rural setting.
articles.trivia: The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission is built on the principles of 'federated architecture' and 'open-source' platforms.

articles.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic All-Weather Tunnel in Himalayas
articles.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced the completion of a crucial all-weather tunnel in the challenging Himalayan terrain, enhancing strategic connectivity.
• The tunnel significantly reduces travel time and ensures year-round access to forward areas, previously cut off during winter months due to heavy snowfall.
• This infrastructure project is vital for rapid troop movement, logistical support, and facilitating economic development in border regions.
• The engineering feat involved overcoming extreme weather conditions and geological challenges, showcasing India's capabilities in high-altitude construction.
• It is part of India's broader strategy to upgrade border infrastructure to meet security requirements and promote regional integration.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: Roads
Linkages between development and spread of extremism
prelimsPoints: BRO is a statutory body under the Ministry of Defence.
Constructs and maintains road networks in border areas and friendly neighboring countries.
Significance of all-weather connectivity for defense and civilian movement.
Examples of other strategic tunnels/passes in Himalayas (e.g., Atal Tunnel).
Geopolitical importance of border infrastructure in the Himalayas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of border infrastructure development, particularly tunnels, in enhancing India's national security and defence preparedness in the Himalayan region.
Analyze the challenges faced by organizations like BRO in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude, geologically sensitive areas.
Examine the socio-economic impact of improved connectivity on border populations and regional development.
Evaluate how such infrastructure projects contribute to India's overall strategy for managing its borders and relations with neighboring countries.
articles.imageDescription: An aerial view of a newly constructed tunnel entrance in a snow-capped mountain range, with military vehicles nearby.
articles.trivia: The BRO was established in 1960 to secure India's borders and develop infrastructure in remote areas.

articles.headline: Election Commission of India to Launch 'Mission Accessibility 2.0' to Enhance Inclusive Voter Participation
articles.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) announced the launch of 'Mission Accessibility 2.0', a comprehensive initiative aimed at ensuring greater inclusivity in the electoral process.
• The mission focuses on making polling stations and voter registration more accessible for persons with disabilities (PwDs), senior citizens, and other marginalized groups.
• Key measures include providing ramps, wheelchairs, sign language interpreters, accessible transport, and simplified registration procedures.
• ECI will also leverage technology for voter education and grievance redressal, ensuring no eligible voter is left behind.
• This initiative reinforces ECI's commitment to strengthening democratic participation and upholding the principle of 'no voter to be left behind'.
gsSubTopics: Salient features of the Representation of People’s Act
Appointment to various Constitutional posts, powers, functions and responsibilities of various Constitutional Bodies
Welfare schemes for vulnerable sections of the population by the Centre and States
prelimsPoints: Constitutional status of ECI (Article 324).
Composition of ECI (Chief Election Commissioner and Election Commissioners).
Functions of ECI: superintendence, direction, control of elections.
Rights of PwDs under RPwD Act, 2016.
Concept of 'ethical voting' and 'informed voting'.
mainsPoints: Discuss the importance of voter accessibility initiatives in strengthening democratic participation and ensuring electoral integrity in India.
Analyze the challenges faced by persons with disabilities and senior citizens in exercising their franchise, and how 'Mission Accessibility 2.0' addresses these.
Evaluate the role of the Election Commission of India in promoting inclusive elections and its efforts beyond traditional election management.
Suggest further reforms or technological interventions that could enhance voter participation among marginalized sections.
articles.imageDescription: A graphic showing a wheelchair-accessible polling booth with diverse voters, including PwDs and senior citizens.
articles.trivia: India's first general election in 1951-52 had over 173 million eligible voters, many of whom were illiterate, requiring innovative voting methods.

articles.headline: India Hosts G20 Working Group Meeting on Digital Public Infrastructure
articles.explainer:
• India hosted a crucial G20 Working Group meeting focused on shaping global standards and cooperation for Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• The discussions centered on leveraging India's successful DPI model (e.g., Aadhaar, UPI) to accelerate digital transformation in developing nations.
• Key themes included interoperability, data governance, cybersecurity frameworks, and ensuring equitable access to digital services.
• Member nations emphasized the need for collaborative efforts to build resilient and inclusive digital ecosystems worldwide.
• India's leadership in this domain aims to foster a shared understanding and common principles for DPI development and deployment across the G20.
gsSubTopics: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests
Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests
Important International institutions, agencies and fora - their structure, mandate
prelimsPoints: G20 is an intergovernmental forum of 19 countries and the European Union.
India's presidency of G20 (2022-2023) focused on 'One Earth, One Family, One Future'.
Examples of India's DPI: Aadhaar, UPI, DigiLocker, CoWIN.
Concept of 'India Stack' - layers of digital infrastructure.
DPI's role in financial inclusion and public service delivery.
mainsPoints: Discuss India's leadership in promoting Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) at global forums like G20, and its potential impact on developing economies.
Analyze the key components and benefits of India's DPI model (India Stack) and its relevance for other nations.
Examine the challenges and ethical considerations associated with the widespread adoption of DPI, including data privacy and digital divide.
Evaluate how international cooperation on DPI can foster inclusive digital transformation and address global developmental disparities.
articles.imageDescription: A G20 logo superimposed on a map of the world, with digital network lines connecting various countries.
articles.trivia: India's Unified Payments Interface (UPI) processes more transactions than credit cards and debit cards combined in many countries.

articles.headline: Government Announces New Policy Framework for Quantum Computing Research & Development
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology unveiled a comprehensive policy framework aimed at accelerating Quantum Computing research and development in India.
• The policy seeks to establish India as a leading nation in quantum technology by fostering indigenous innovation, talent development, and industry collaboration.
• Key pillars include significant funding for research institutes, setting up national quantum labs, and creating a robust ecosystem for startups.
• Focus areas span quantum communication, quantum sensing, quantum cryptography, and quantum materials science.
• This strategic push is critical for national security, economic competitiveness, and solving complex problems in healthcare, finance, and AI.
gsSubTopics: Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.
prelimsPoints: Quantum computing uses principles of quantum mechanics (superposition, entanglement).
Qubit is the basic unit of quantum information.
Potential applications: drug discovery, materials science, financial modeling, AI, cryptography.
National Quantum Mission (NQM) launched in 2023.
Difference between classical and quantum computing.
mainsPoints: Discuss the transformative potential of quantum computing and its strategic importance for India's technological future and national security.
Analyze the key provisions of India's new policy framework for quantum computing, including its focus on research, talent, and ecosystem development.
Examine the challenges and ethical considerations associated with the advancement of quantum technology, such as cybersecurity implications.
Evaluate how India can leverage its scientific talent and existing IT infrastructure to become a global leader in quantum computing.
articles.imageDescription: An abstract illustration depicting quantum bits (qubits) and interconnected nodes, symbolizing quantum computing.
articles.trivia: A quantum computer with 300 qubits could store more information than the number of atoms in the observable universe.

articles.headline: Conservationists Raise Alarm Over Decline in Great Indian Bustard Population
articles.explainer:
• Wildlife conservationists and environmental NGOs have expressed grave concerns over the alarming decline in the population of the Great Indian Bustard (GIB).
• The GIB, a critically endangered species, faces existential threats primarily from habitat loss, power lines, and poaching.
• Despite conservation efforts, including captive breeding programs and habitat protection, the numbers continue to dwindle, pushing the species closer to extinction.
• Experts are calling for more stringent enforcement of protective measures, relocation of critical power infrastructure, and increased community involvement.
• The GIB is a flagship species for grassland ecosystems, and its decline indicates broader ecological degradation in its habitats.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and Environment
prelimsPoints: GIB is the State bird of Rajasthan.
Critically Endangered status on IUCN Red List.
Found primarily in Rajasthan, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh.
Threats: power lines (collision), habitat degradation, hunting.
Wildlife Protection Act, 1972: Schedule I species.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Great Indian Bustard and the factors contributing to its alarming population decline.
Analyze the effectiveness of current conservation strategies, including captive breeding and habitat protection, for the GIB.
Examine the conflict between infrastructure development (e.g., power lines) and wildlife conservation, suggesting sustainable solutions.
Evaluate the role of community participation and public awareness in successful wildlife conservation efforts for critically endangered species like the GIB.
articles.imageDescription: A photograph of a majestic Great Indian Bustard standing in a dry grassland habitat.
articles.trivia: The Great Indian Bustard is one of the heaviest flying birds, with males weighing up to 15 kg.

articles.headline: RBI Signals Cautious Stance on Interest Rates Amidst Inflationary Pressures
articles.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) indicated a cautious approach towards interest rate adjustments in its latest monetary policy review, citing persistent inflationary pressures.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of moderation, the RBI emphasized the need to remain vigilant against potential external shocks and domestic supply-side issues.
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts anticipate that the RBI will likely maintain the current repo rate in the near term, closely monitoring global economic developments and domestic price stability.
• This stance aims to ensure macroeconomic stability and manage liquidity in the financial system effectively.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI is India's central bank and monetary authority.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines policy rates.
Primary objective of MPC: maintain price stability while keeping growth in mind.
Repo Rate: rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks.
CPI (Consumer Price Index) is the main measure of retail inflation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the current challenges faced by the Reserve Bank of India in managing inflation while supporting economic growth.
Analyze the tools and strategies employed by the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) to achieve its twin objectives of price stability and growth.
Examine the impact of global economic factors and supply-side disruptions on India's inflation trajectory and RBI's policy decisions.
Evaluate the implications of RBI's cautious stance on interest rates for various sectors of the Indian economy, including lending, investment, and consumption.
articles.imageDescription: A close-up of the Reserve Bank of India's logo, symbolizing monetary authority and economic stability.
articles.trivia: The RBI was established in 1935 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and was nationalized in 1949.

articles.headline: President Awards 'Pradhan Mantri Rashtriya Bal Puraskar' to Young Innovators
articles.explainer:
• The President of India conferred the 'Pradhan Mantri Rashtriya Bal Puraskar' (PMRBP) upon exceptional children for their outstanding achievements.
• The awards recognize children aged 5-18 years for excellence in innovation, scholastic achievements, sports, arts & culture, social service, and bravery.
• This year's recipients were lauded for their groundbreaking inventions, academic prowess, and courageous acts, inspiring their peers and the nation.
• The ceremony, held annually around Republic Day, aims to encourage young talent and foster a culture of excellence and selfless service among children.
• The award underscores the government's commitment to recognizing and nurturing the potential of India's youth, who are future nation-builders.
gsSubTopics: Role of women and women’s organization, population and associated issues, poverty and developmental issues, urbanization, their problems and their remedies.
Social empowerment
articles.source: Ministry of Women and Child Development
prelimsPoints: PMRBP is conferred by the President of India.
Awards categories: Innovation, Scholastic, Sports, Arts & Culture, Social Service, Bravery.
Administered by the Ministry of Women and Child Development.
Given annually around Republic Day (January 26).
Significance: recognizing and encouraging young talent.
articles.imageDescription: The President of India presenting an award to a young child on a stage, with an audience applauding.
articles.trivia: The award was previously known as the National Child Awards for Exceptional Achievement before being rebranded in 2018.

articles.headline: Union Cabinet Approves Amendments to Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act
articles.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has approved significant amendments to the Multi-State Cooperative Societies (MSCS) Act, aiming to enhance governance, management, and financial health of cooperative bodies.
• The proposed amendments introduce provisions for electoral reforms, regular auditing, and stricter financial discipline to ensure transparency and accountability.
• The move is intended to strengthen the cooperative movement in India, which plays a crucial role in agriculture, dairy, credit, and housing sectors.
• Measures include setting up a Cooperative Election Authority and a Cooperative Rehabilitation, Reconstruction and Development Fund.
• These reforms are expected to instill greater trust among members and attract investment, leading to more robust and professionally managed cooperatives.
gsSubTopics: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors and issues arising out of their design and implementation
Role of NGOs, SHGs, various groups and associations, donors, charities, institutional and other stakeholders
prelimsPoints: Cooperative societies are covered under State List (Entry 32 of List II).
Multi-State Cooperative Societies operate in more than one state, governed by MSCS Act.
97th Constitutional Amendment Act, 2011, gave constitutional status to cooperatives.
Ministry of Cooperation was created in 2021.
Examples: AMUL, IFFCO, KRIBHCO.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the cooperative movement in India's socio-economic development, particularly in rural areas.
Analyze the key challenges faced by cooperative societies in India, especially regarding governance, financial management, and political interference.
Examine how the proposed amendments to the Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act aim to address these issues and strengthen the cooperative sector.
Evaluate the potential impact of these reforms on transparency, accountability, and the overall growth of cooperatives in India.
articles.imageDescription: A group of farmers discussing in front of a cooperative society building, symbolizing community and collaboration.
articles.trivia: The 97th Constitutional Amendment Act inserted Article 43B (Promotion of Cooperative Societies) as a DPSP and added Part IXB (The Cooperative Societies) to the Constitution.

articles.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Discovers Ancient Buddhist Relics in Odisha
articles.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant ancient Buddhist relics during excavations in Odisha's Jajpur district.
• The findings include remnants of stupas, monastic complexes, and intricate sculptures, dating back to the early medieval period.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread and flourishing of Buddhism in eastern India, particularly in the Kalinga region.
• The site is expected to be a major archaeological interest, providing valuable insights into the architectural styles and religious practices of the era.
• The ASI plans further extensive excavations and conservation efforts to preserve and study these invaluable historical artifacts.
gsSubTopics: Indian culture will cover the salient aspects of Art Forms, Literature and Architecture from ancient to modern times.

History of the World will include events from 18th century such as industrial revolution, world wars, redrawal of national boundaries, colonization, decolonization, political philosophies like communism, capitalism, socialism etc.- their forms and effect on the society.
prelimsPoints: ASI is the premier organization for archaeological research and protection of cultural heritage.
Buddhism's spread in Eastern India (Odisha, Bihar, Bengal).
Significance of Jajpur district in Odisha (Udayagiri, Lalitgiri, Ratnagiri Buddhist sites).
Components of a stupa (anda, harmika, chatra).
Major Buddhist councils and patrons.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of the newly discovered Buddhist relics in Odisha in understanding the history and spread of Buddhism in Eastern India.
Analyze the architectural and artistic features of Buddhist art and architecture from the early medieval period, with reference to the Kalinga region.
Examine the role of the Archaeological Survey of India in preserving and promoting India's rich cultural heritage.
Evaluate how such archaeological discoveries contribute to our understanding of India's ancient past and its religious diversity.
articles.imageDescription: Excavated Buddhist stupa ruins with intricate carvings, partially covered by earth, at an archaeological site.
articles.trivia: The famous Kalinga War fought by Emperor Ashoka, which led to his conversion to Buddhism, took place in ancient Kalinga (modern-day Odisha).

articles.headline: Indian Navy Conducts Major Naval Exercise in Indian Ocean Region
articles.explainer:
• The Indian Navy concluded a large-scale naval exercise in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR), demonstrating its operational readiness and strategic capabilities.
• The exercise involved a wide array of naval assets, including aircraft carriers, submarines, destroyers, and maritime patrol aircraft.
• Key objectives included enhancing interoperability, validating maritime doctrines, and rehearsing complex naval maneuvers in a multi-threat environment.
• The drill also focused on maritime domain awareness, anti-submarine warfare, and humanitarian assistance/disaster relief operations.
• This exercise underscores India's commitment to being a net security provider in the IOR and safeguarding its maritime interests.

Various Security forces and agencies and their mandate
prelimsPoints: Indian Navy's role in safeguarding India's maritime interests and trade routes.

Bilateral and multilateral naval exercises (e.g., Malabar, Milan).
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic significance of the Indian Ocean Region for India's security and economic interests.
Analyze the role of the Indian Navy in projecting India's power and ensuring maritime security in the IOR.

articles.imageDescription: Indian Navy warships and aircraft conducting maneuvers at sea during a large-scale exercise.
articles.trivia: The Indian Navy's motto is 'Sham No Varunah', meaning 'May the Lord of the Oceans be auspicious unto us'.

articles.headline: Government Boosts MSP for Rabi Crops Ahead of Procurement Season
articles.explainer:
• The Union Government announced an increase in Minimum Support Prices (MSP) for all mandated Rabi crops for the upcoming marketing season.
• The hike aims to ensure remunerative prices for farmers, encourage crop diversification, and address concerns over rising input costs.
• The decision is expected to provide a significant boost to farmers' income, particularly for wheat, barley, and mustard growers.
• The government reiterated its commitment to supporting farmers and ensuring food security through stable agricultural policies.
• This move is part of broader efforts to achieve the target of doubling farmers' income and making agriculture more sustainable.
gsSubTopics: Major crops cropping patterns in various parts of the country, different types of irrigation and irrigation system storage, transport and marketing of agricultural produce and issues and related constraints; e-technology in the aid of farmers.

Issues related to direct and indirect farm subsidies and minimum support prices; Public Distribution System- objectives, functioning, limitations, revamping; issues of buffer stocks and food security; Technology missions; economics of animal-rearing.
keyTerms: Commission for Agricultural Costs and Prices (CACP)
prelimsPoints: MSP is a 'minimum' price set by the Government for certain agricultural products.
CACP recommends MSPs, approved by Cabinet Committee on Economic Affairs (CCEA).
Rabi crops are sown in winter (Oct-Dec) and harvested in spring (Apr-June). Examples: wheat, barley, gram, mustard.
Kharif crops are sown in monsoon (June-July) and harvested in autumn (Sept-Oct). Examples: rice, maize, jowar.
Objectives of MSP: support farmers, ensure food security, stabilize prices.
mainsPoints: Discuss the rationale behind the Minimum Support Price (MSP) policy in India and its role in ensuring farmers' welfare and food security.

articles.headline: National Voters' Day Celebrated with Emphasis on Informed and Ethical Voting
articles.explainer:
• National Voters' Day was celebrated across India on January 25, 2025, with the theme 'Nothing Like Voting, I Vote For Sure'.
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) organized various events to raise awareness among voters, especially youth, about their electoral rights and responsibilities.
• The focus was on promoting informed, ethical, and participative voting to strengthen democratic values.
• New eligible voters were felicitated and provided with their Electors Photo Identity Cards (EPICs).
• The day serves as a reminder of the importance of every citizen's participation in shaping the nation's future.

highlights: Government unveils ambitious National Infrastructure & Logistics Corridor as part of 'Viksit Bharat 2047' vision.
Supreme Court to re-examine the scope and implementation of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) for mega projects.
India and France conclude Strategic Dialogue, pledging enhanced cooperation in defence, space, and emerging technologies.
ISRO successfully conducts hot test of a next-generation cryogenic engine, crucial for future heavy-lift launch vehicles.
Economic Survey 2024-25 projects a robust 7% GDP growth for India, while cautioning about global economic headwinds.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the 'Viksit Bharat 2047' vision and related initiatives, consider the following statements:
1. The National Infrastructure & Logistics Corridor project aims to reduce India's logistics costs to below 5% of GDP.
2. Public-Private Partnerships (PPPs) are envisioned as a key financing model for this project.
3. The project focuses solely on road and rail connectivity to boost manufacturing.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
options: (a) 1 and 2 only
(b) 2 only
(c) 1 and 3 only
(d) 1, 2 and 3
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is incorrect. While the project aims to reduce logistics costs, the immediate target is often cited as below 10% of GDP by 2030, not 5% in the context of 'Viksit Bharat 2047' which looks further ahead. Statement 2 is correct, as PPPs are crucial for large-scale infrastructure projects. Statement 3 is incorrect; the project is described as multi-modal, including high-speed rail, dedicated freight corridors, national highways, and greenfield industrial cities, not solely road and rail, and it also connects industrial hubs and ports.

prelimsMocks.question: Regarding the Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) in India, which of the following statements is NOT correct?
options: (a) EIA is a statutory requirement under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.
(b) Public consultation is a mandatory step in the EIA process for all categories of projects.
(c) Expert Appraisal Committees (EACs) play a crucial role in the appraisal of projects requiring environmental clearance.
(d) The Supreme Court of India has often intervened to strengthen environmental governance, including aspects of EIA.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) Public consultation is a mandatory step in the EIA process for all categories of projects.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement (b) is NOT correct. Public consultation is a mandatory step in the EIA process, but it is not required for all categories of projects. Certain projects, particularly those categorized as 'B2' (smaller scale) or strategic projects, are often exempted from the public hearing process. Statements (a), (c), and (d) are correct.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements about India-France relations:
1. France was the first P5 nation to support India's claim for a permanent seat in the UN Security Council.
2. Varuna is a joint air exercise between India and France.
3. Both countries are collaborating on the International Thermonuclear Experimental Reactor (ITER) project.
How many of the above statements are correct?
options: (a) Only one
(b) Only two
(c) All three
(d) None
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct. France has consistently supported India's UNSC permanent membership. Statement 2 is incorrect. Varuna is a joint naval exercise between India and France. Garuda is the joint air exercise. Statement 3 is correct. India and France are both members of the ITER project, which aims to demonstrate the scientific and technological feasibility of fusion power.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following statements correctly describes a 'Green Hydrogen' production method?
options: (a) Hydrogen produced from natural gas with carbon capture and storage.
(b) Hydrogen produced from coal gasification.
(c) Hydrogen produced via electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
(d) Hydrogen produced as a by-product of industrial chemical processes.
prelimsMocks.answer: (c) Hydrogen produced via electrolysis of water using renewable energy sources.
prelimsMocks.explanation: Green hydrogen refers specifically to hydrogen produced through the electrolysis of water, powered by renewable energy sources like solar or wind. (a) describes 'blue hydrogen', (b) 'grey hydrogen', and (d) can be 'grey' or 'pink' depending on the source.

prelimsMocks.question: With reference to the Reserve Bank of India (RBI) and its monetary policy, which of the following is the primary objective of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC)?
options: (a) To stabilize the exchange rate of the Indian Rupee.
(b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
(c) To manage government debt and fiscal deficit.
(d) To promote financial inclusion and expand banking services.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To maintain price stability while keeping in mind the objective of growth.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary objective of the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) is to maintain price stability, with the target for retail inflation set at 4% with a band of +/- 2%. While other options are important functions or goals for the RBI, maintaining price stability (inflation targeting) is the primary objective of the MPC, as explicitly stated in the RBI Act, 1934 (as amended).

mainsMocks.question: The 'Viksit Bharat 2047' vision necessitates a multi-faceted approach encompassing robust infrastructure development and significant human capital enhancement. Discuss how the proposed National Infrastructure & Logistics Corridor aligns with this vision and what further steps are needed to realize India's demographic dividend.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development in various sectors; GS3: Infrastructure; Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce 'Viksit Bharat 2047' and the Corridor project. Explain how the corridor boosts economic growth, reduces logistics costs, and creates jobs. Then, discuss the broader human capital requirements: education, skill development, health, and gender equality. Conclude by linking infrastructure and human capital as synergistic for holistic national development.

mainsMocks.question: The Supreme Court's decision to revisit the scope of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) for mega projects highlights the ongoing tension between development and environmental protection. Analyze the role of judicial activism in environmental governance in India, referencing the need for a balanced and effective EIA regime.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Government policies and interventions for development; Statutory, regulatory and various quasi-judicial bodies; GS3: Environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
mainsMocks.approach: Define judicial activism in environmental context. Discuss how courts have filled policy gaps and enforced environmental laws. Explain the EIA process, its importance, and current deficiencies (e.g., public consultation, post-clearance monitoring). Argue for a balanced approach that ensures robust environmental safeguards without unduly stifling essential development, proposing reforms for a more effective EIA regime.

mainsMocks.question: India's strategic partnerships, such as with France, are crucial in navigating a multipolar world and securing its foreign policy objectives. Evaluate the significance of the India-France strategic partnership in promoting India's strategic autonomy and contributing to regional and global security.
mainsMocks.syllabusMatch: GS2: Bilateral, regional and global groupings and agreements involving India and/or affecting India’s interests; Effect of policies and politics of developed and developing countries on India’s interests.
mainsMocks.approach: Introduce India's foreign policy goals in a multipolar world and the concept of strategic autonomy. Detail the historical and contemporary pillars of India-France partnership (defence, space, nuclear, counter-terrorism, Indo-Pacific). Discuss how this partnership bolsters India's defense capabilities, technological self-reliance, and diplomatic influence. Conclude on its role in balancing power and addressing common challenges.

GS1.headline: President Awards 'Pradhan Mantri Rashtriya Bal Puraskar' to Young Innovators
GS1.explainer:
• The President of India conferred the 'Pradhan Mantri Rashtriya Bal Puraskar' (PMRBP) upon exceptional children for their outstanding achievements.
• The awards recognize children aged 5-18 years for excellence in innovation, scholastic achievements, sports, arts & culture, social service, and bravery.
• This year's recipients were lauded for their groundbreaking inventions, academic prowess, and courageous acts, inspiring their peers and the nation.
• The ceremony, held annually around Republic Day, aims to encourage young talent and foster a culture of excellence and selfless service among children.
• The award underscores the government's commitment to recognizing and nurturing the potential of India's youth, who are future nation-builders.

GS1.headline: Archaeological Survey of India Discovers Ancient Buddhist Relics in Odisha
GS1.explainer:
• The Archaeological Survey of India (ASI) announced the discovery of significant ancient Buddhist relics during excavations in Odisha's Jajpur district.
• The findings include remnants of stupas, monastic complexes, and intricate sculptures, dating back to the early medieval period.
• This discovery sheds new light on the spread and flourishing of Buddhism in eastern India, particularly in the Kalinga region.
• The site is expected to be a major archaeological interest, providing valuable insights into the architectural styles and religious practices of the era.
• The ASI plans further extensive excavations and conservation efforts to preserve and study these invaluable historical artifacts.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Bench to Revisit Scope of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) for Mega Projects
GS2.explainer:
• A special bench of the Supreme Court has decided to re-examine the legal framework and implementation of Environmental Impact Assessments (EIA) for major infrastructure and industrial projects.
• The move comes amidst growing concerns over the efficacy of current EIA processes in balancing development with environmental protection.
• Key issues to be addressed include public consultation mechanisms, post-clearance compliance monitoring, and the role of expert appraisal committees.
• Environmental activists and legal experts have welcomed the decision, hoping for stronger safeguards against ecological degradation.
• The ruling could lead to significant reforms in India's environmental governance and project approval procedures.

GS2.imageDescription: A gavel resting on legal documents, symbolizing a judicial review of environmental policies.
GS2.trivia: The first formal EIA notification in India was issued in 1994, under the Environment (Protection) Act, 1986.

GS2.headline: India-France Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Enhanced Defence & Space Cooperation
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-France Strategic Dialogue concluded in Paris, building on the momentum from recent high-level visits, including the Republic Day guest.
• Both nations reaffirmed their commitment to a robust strategic partnership, focusing on deepening cooperation in defence, space, and emerging technologies.
• Discussions included joint military exercises, co-development of defence platforms, and intelligence sharing to counter regional security threats.
• In space, new agreements were signed for collaboration on satellite navigation, earth observation, and human spaceflight missions.
• The dialogue also emphasized strengthening bilateral ties in trade, climate action, and multilateral forums like the UN Security Council.

GS2.headline: Health Ministry Expands 'Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission' to Remote Rural Areas
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Health Ministry announced a significant expansion of the Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) to cover previously underserved remote rural areas.
• The initiative aims to create a seamless online platform for healthcare services, enabling digital health records, teleconsultations, and paperless prescriptions.
• Special emphasis is being placed on training local community health workers and equipping primary health centers with necessary digital infrastructure.
• The expansion seeks to bridge the urban-rural divide in healthcare access and improve the efficiency of health service delivery.
• This move is crucial for achieving universal health coverage and improving public health outcomes across the nation.

GS2.headline: Election Commission of India to Launch 'Mission Accessibility 2.0' to Enhance Inclusive Voter Participation
GS2.explainer:
• The Election Commission of India (ECI) announced the launch of 'Mission Accessibility 2.0', a comprehensive initiative aimed at ensuring greater inclusivity in the electoral process.
• The mission focuses on making polling stations and voter registration more accessible for persons with disabilities (PwDs), senior citizens, and other marginalized groups.
• Key measures include providing ramps, wheelchairs, sign language interpreters, accessible transport, and simplified registration procedures.
• ECI will also leverage technology for voter education and grievance redressal, ensuring no eligible voter is left behind.
• This initiative reinforces ECI's commitment to strengthening democratic participation and upholding the principle of 'no voter to be left behind'.

GS2.headline: India Hosts G20 Working Group Meeting on Digital Public Infrastructure
GS2.explainer:
• India hosted a crucial G20 Working Group meeting focused on shaping global standards and cooperation for Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI).
• The discussions centered on leveraging India's successful DPI model (e.g., Aadhaar, UPI) to accelerate digital transformation in developing nations.
• Key themes included interoperability, data governance, cybersecurity frameworks, and ensuring equitable access to digital services.
• Member nations emphasized the need for collaborative efforts to build resilient and inclusive digital ecosystems worldwide.
• India's leadership in this domain aims to foster a shared understanding and common principles for DPI development and deployment across the G20.

GS2.headline: Union Cabinet Approves Amendments to Multi-State Cooperative Societies Act
GS2.explainer:
• The Union Cabinet has approved significant amendments to the Multi-State Cooperative Societies (MSCS) Act, aiming to enhance governance, management, and financial health of cooperative bodies.
• The proposed amendments introduce provisions for electoral reforms, regular auditing, and stricter financial discipline to ensure transparency and accountability.
• The move is intended to strengthen the cooperative movement in India, which plays a crucial role in agriculture, dairy, credit, and housing sectors.
• Measures include setting up a Cooperative Election Authority and a Cooperative Rehabilitation, Reconstruction and Development Fund.
• These reforms are expected to instill greater trust among members and attract investment, leading to more robust and professionally managed cooperatives.

GS3.trivia: India aims to bring down its logistics costs from the current 13-14% of GDP to less than 10% by 2030, a key objective of such corridor projects.

GS3.headline: ISRO Successfully Tests Next-Gen Cryogenic Engine for Future Heavy-Lift Missions
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) announced the successful hot test of a new, more powerful cryogenic engine at its Propulsion Complex in Mahendragiri.
• This advanced engine is designed to power the upper stages of India's next generation heavy-lift launch vehicles, significantly enhancing payload capacity.
• The successful test marks a crucial step towards developing more efficient and cost-effective access to space for India.
• It will be instrumental for future missions, including advanced communication satellites, deep-space probes, and potentially crewed missions beyond low-Earth orbit.
• The development underscores India's growing self-reliance and technological prowess in complex rocketry and space propulsion systems.
gsSubTopics: Achievements of Indians in science & technology; indigenization of technology and developing new technology
Awareness in the fields of Space
prelimsPoints: Cryogenic engines use liquid oxygen and liquid hydrogen as propellants.
ISRO's previous cryogenic engine was used in GSLV Mk III (LVM3).
Significance for heavier satellites and interplanetary missions.
Mahendragiri: ISRO Propulsion Complex (IPRC) location.
Difference between solid, liquid, and cryogenic propellants.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of indigenous cryogenic engine development for India's space program and strategic autonomy.
Analyze how enhanced heavy-lift capabilities will impact India's role in global space economy and exploration.
Examine the technological challenges involved in cryogenic engine development and how ISRO has overcome them.
Evaluate the potential applications of this new engine for future Indian space missions, including human spaceflight and deep-space exploration.
GS3.imageDescription: An illustration of a cryogenic engine firing, with flames emanating from the nozzle against a dark background.
GS3.trivia: India is one of only a handful of nations to have successfully developed and deployed cryogenic rocket technology.

GS3.headline: Economic Survey 2024-25 Projects Robust 7% Growth, Highlights Global Headwinds
GS3.explainer:
• The Economic Survey 2024-25, tabled ahead of the Union Budget, projected a robust 7% GDP growth for the upcoming fiscal year.
• The Survey highlighted strong domestic demand, government capital expenditure, and a resilient services sector as key growth drivers.
• It also cautioned about persistent global headwinds, including geopolitical tensions, commodity price volatility, and slower global trade growth.
• Recommendations included continued focus on structural reforms, fiscal consolidation, and boosting private investment to sustain growth trajectory.
• The document emphasized the need for targeted policies to address inflation, create employment, and ensure equitable development.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Inclusive growth and issues arising from it
prelimsPoints: Economic Survey is prepared by the Chief Economic Adviser and presented before the Budget.
It reviews the economic performance of the past year and provides an outlook for the future.
Key indicators: GDP growth, inflation, fiscal deficit, CAD, FDI.
Difference between nominal and real GDP.
Components of GDP: C+I+G+(X-M).
mainsPoints: Analyze the key drivers and challenges to India's economic growth as highlighted by the Economic Survey 2024-25.
Discuss the policy recommendations of the Survey, particularly regarding fiscal management and structural reforms, in achieving sustainable and inclusive growth.
Evaluate the impact of global economic uncertainties and geopolitical developments on India's growth trajectory.
Examine the role of government capital expenditure and private investment in boosting economic activity and employment generation.
GS3.imageDescription: A chart showing an upward trend of India's GDP growth, with economic indicators in the background.
GS3.trivia: The first Economic Survey was presented in 1950-51 and was presented along with the Union Budget until 1964, after which it was delinked.

GS3.headline: India Launches National Mission on Green Hydrogen for Industrial Decarbonisation
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy officially launched the National Green Hydrogen Mission, targeting industrial decarbonisation and energy security.
• The mission aims to make India a global hub for green hydrogen production and export, leveraging its renewable energy potential.
• Key objectives include developing domestic manufacturing capabilities for electrolysers, promoting research and development, and creating demand for green hydrogen in sectors like refineries, fertilizers, and steel.
• Significant financial incentives and policy support will be provided for pilot projects and scaling up production capacity.
• This initiative is crucial for India to meet its climate change commitments and achieve net-zero emissions by 2070.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Science and Technology- developments and their applications and effects in everyday life
Infrastructure: Energy

India's target for green hydrogen production.
Role of National Green Hydrogen Mission in achieving climate targets.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of green hydrogen for India's energy security and climate goals.
Analyze the potential benefits and challenges in establishing India as a global hub for green hydrogen production and export.
Examine the policy measures and technological advancements required to scale up green hydrogen adoption in key industrial sectors.
Evaluate the economic and environmental impact of the National Green Hydrogen Mission on India's energy transition.
GS3.imageDescription: An illustration depicting a green hydrogen production plant with wind turbines and solar panels in the background.
GS3.trivia: Hydrogen is the most abundant chemical substance in the universe, but it rarely exists as a free element on Earth.

GS3.headline: Border Roads Organisation Completes Strategic All-Weather Tunnel in Himalayas
GS3.explainer:
• The Border Roads Organisation (BRO) announced the completion of a crucial all-weather tunnel in the challenging Himalayan terrain, enhancing strategic connectivity.
• The tunnel significantly reduces travel time and ensures year-round access to forward areas, previously cut off during winter months due to heavy snowfall.
• This infrastructure project is vital for rapid troop movement, logistical support, and facilitating economic development in border regions.
• The engineering feat involved overcoming extreme weather conditions and geological challenges, showcasing India's capabilities in high-altitude construction.
• It is part of India's broader strategy to upgrade border infrastructure to meet security requirements and promote regional integration.
gsSubTopics: Security challenges and their management in border areas
Infrastructure: Roads
Linkages between development and spread of extremism

Constructs and maintains road networks in border areas and friendly neighboring countries.

Geopolitical importance of border infrastructure in the Himalayas.
mainsPoints: Discuss the strategic importance of border infrastructure development, particularly tunnels, in enhancing India's national security and defence preparedness in the Himalayan region.
Analyze the challenges faced by organizations like BRO in constructing infrastructure in high-altitude, geologically sensitive areas.
Examine the socio-economic impact of improved connectivity on border populations and regional development.
Evaluate how such infrastructure projects contribute to India's overall strategy for managing its borders and relations with neighboring countries.
GS3.imageDescription: An aerial view of a newly constructed tunnel entrance in a snow-capped mountain range, with military vehicles nearby.
GS3.trivia: The BRO was established in 1960 to secure India's borders and develop infrastructure in remote areas.

GS3.headline: Government Announces New Policy Framework for Quantum Computing Research & Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Science & Technology unveiled a comprehensive policy framework aimed at accelerating Quantum Computing research and development in India.
• The policy seeks to establish India as a leading nation in quantum technology by fostering indigenous innovation, talent development, and industry collaboration.
• Key pillars include significant funding for research institutes, setting up national quantum labs, and creating a robust ecosystem for startups.
• Focus areas span quantum communication, quantum sensing, quantum cryptography, and quantum materials science.
• This strategic push is critical for national security, economic competitiveness, and solving complex problems in healthcare, finance, and AI.

Awareness in the fields of IT, Space, Computers, robotics, nano-technology, bio-technology and issues relating to intellectual property rights.

Examine the challenges and ethical considerations associated with the advancement of quantum technology, such as cybersecurity implications.
Evaluate how India can leverage its scientific talent and existing IT infrastructure to become a global leader in quantum computing.
GS3.imageDescription: An abstract illustration depicting quantum bits (qubits) and interconnected nodes, symbolizing quantum computing.
GS3.trivia: A quantum computer with 300 qubits could store more information than the number of atoms in the observable universe.

GS3.headline: Conservationists Raise Alarm Over Decline in Great Indian Bustard Population
GS3.explainer:
• Wildlife conservationists and environmental NGOs have expressed grave concerns over the alarming decline in the population of the Great Indian Bustard (GIB).
• The GIB, a critically endangered species, faces existential threats primarily from habitat loss, power lines, and poaching.
• Despite conservation efforts, including captive breeding programs and habitat protection, the numbers continue to dwindle, pushing the species closer to extinction.
• Experts are calling for more stringent enforcement of protective measures, relocation of critical power infrastructure, and increased community involvement.
• The GIB is a flagship species for grassland ecosystems, and its decline indicates broader ecological degradation in its habitats.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment
Biodiversity and Environment
prelimsPoints: GIB is the State bird of Rajasthan.
Critically Endangered status on IUCN Red List.
Found primarily in Rajasthan, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh.
Threats: power lines (collision), habitat degradation, hunting.
Wildlife Protection Act, 1972: Schedule I species.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Great Indian Bustard and the factors contributing to its alarming population decline.
Analyze the effectiveness of current conservation strategies, including captive breeding and habitat protection, for the GIB.
Examine the conflict between infrastructure development (e.g., power lines) and wildlife conservation, suggesting sustainable solutions.
Evaluate the role of community participation and public awareness in successful wildlife conservation efforts for critically endangered species like the GIB.
GS3.imageDescription: A photograph of a majestic Great Indian Bustard standing in a dry grassland habitat.
GS3.trivia: The Great Indian Bustard is one of the heaviest flying birds, with males weighing up to 15 kg.

GS3.headline: RBI Signals Cautious Stance on Interest Rates Amidst Inflationary Pressures
GS3.explainer:
• The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) indicated a cautious approach towards interest rate adjustments in its latest monetary policy review, citing persistent inflationary pressures.
• While retail inflation has shown signs of moderation, the RBI emphasized the need to remain vigilant against potential external shocks and domestic supply-side issues.
• The Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) reiterated its commitment to aligning inflation with the target while supporting growth.
• Analysts anticipate that the RBI will likely maintain the current repo rate in the near term, closely monitoring global economic developments and domestic price stability.
• This stance aims to ensure macroeconomic stability and manage liquidity in the financial system effectively.
gsSubTopics: Indian Economy and issues relating to planning, mobilization of resources, growth, development and employment
Government Budgeting
Monetary policy
prelimsPoints: RBI is India's central bank and monetary authority.
Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) determines policy rates.
Primary objective of MPC: maintain price stability while keeping growth in mind.
Repo Rate: rate at which RBI lends to commercial banks.
CPI (Consumer Price Index) is the main measure of retail inflation.
mainsPoints: Discuss the current challenges faced by the Reserve Bank of India in managing inflation while supporting economic growth.

Examine the impact of global economic factors and supply-side disruptions on India's inflation trajectory and RBI's policy decisions.

GS3.imageDescription: A close-up of the Reserve Bank of India's logo, symbolizing monetary authority and economic stability.
GS3.trivia: The RBI was established in 1935 under the Reserve Bank of India Act, 1934, and was nationalized in 1949.

GS3.headline: Indian Navy Conducts Major Naval Exercise in Indian Ocean Region
GS3.explainer:
• The Indian Navy concluded a large-scale naval exercise in the Indian Ocean Region (IOR), demonstrating its operational readiness and strategic capabilities.
• The exercise involved a wide array of naval assets, including aircraft carriers, submarines, destroyers, and maritime patrol aircraft.
• Key objectives included enhancing interoperability, validating maritime doctrines, and rehearsing complex naval maneuvers in a multi-threat environment.
• The drill also focused on maritime domain awareness, anti-submarine warfare, and humanitarian assistance/disaster relief operations.
• This exercise underscores India's commitment to being a net security provider in the IOR and safeguarding its maritime interests.

GS3.imageDescription: Indian Navy warships and aircraft conducting maneuvers at sea during a large-scale exercise.
GS3.trivia: The Indian Navy's motto is 'Sham No Varunah', meaning 'May the Lord of the Oceans be auspicious unto us'.

GS3.headline: Government Boosts MSP for Rabi Crops Ahead of Procurement Season
GS3.explainer:
• The Union Government announced an increase in Minimum Support Prices (MSP) for all mandated Rabi crops for the upcoming marketing season.
• The hike aims to ensure remunerative prices for farmers, encourage crop diversification, and address concerns over rising input costs.
• The decision is expected to provide a significant boost to farmers' income, particularly for wheat, barley, and mustard growers.
• The government reiterated its commitment to supporting farmers and ensuring food security through stable agricultural policies.
• This move is part of broader efforts to achieve the target of doubling farmers' income and making agriculture more sustainable.

Kharif crops are sown in monsoon (June-July) and harvested in autumn (Sept-Oct). Examples: rice, maize, jowar.
Objectives of MSP: support farmers, ensure food security, stabilize prices.
